title: California's 45th Congressional District election, 2026
description: California's 45th Congressional District race in 2026, centered on incumbent Democrat Derek Tran and the Republican field following former Rep. Michelle Steel's reported decision not to run again.

polling.pollster: USC / Cal State Long Beach / Cal Poly Pomona

polling_note: No public polling found for this race in the last 90 days as of 2026-04-07.

candidates.name: Derek Tran
candidates.summary: Derek Tran is the U.S. representative for California’s 45th Congressional District and is seeking re-election in 2026. According to his House biography, he is an Army veteran, attorney, and small business owner who was born and raised in Los Angeles County and has represented the district since 2025.

career_history.title: Attorney
career_history.description: Practiced law before election to Congress.

career_history.title: U.S. Army veteran
career_history.description: Served in the Army before entering politics.

candidates.donor_summary: Raised approximately $6.44M in the 2023–2024 cycle, with the largest industry support coming from lawyers/law firms ($904,112), followed by retired donors ($525,372), Democratic/liberal interests ($418,522), securities & investment ($377,148), and leadership PACs ($268,100). The top disclosed contributor was JStreetPAC at $65,532, and large individual contributions made up 56.47% of funds. Source data is available on OpenSecrets and the FEC candidate page.
candidates.voting_summary: Tran is an incumbent U.S. Representative and has served since Jan. 3, 2025. GovTrack shows he voted yea on the major roll calls it tracked in 2025–2026, including the Law-Enforcement Innovate to De-Escalate Act, SHOWER Act, AGOA Extension Act, and several D.C. criminal justice measures; GovTrack also reports he missed 1 of 470 roll call votes (0.2%) from Jan. 2025 to Mar. 2026.

links.title: Rep. Derek Tran - Campaign Finance Summary - OpenSecrets

Healthcare.stance: Tran says healthcare should be a right, not a privilege. He supports defending Medicare, opposing Medicaid cuts, and working to lower the cost of prescription drugs and other care.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress

Economy.stance: Tran says he stands with workers and supports a living wage, stronger workplace protections, safer working conditions, and policies that help families afford housing and retirement.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress

Climate/Energy.stance: Tran says the United States should bring back good-paying jobs in clean energy and advanced manufacturing. His campaign also says he supports clean energy, clean water, and clean air.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress

Reproductive Rights.stance: Tran says he is 100% pro-choice and supports protecting Planned Parenthood funding, birth control access, and other women’s health services.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress

Guns & Safety.stance: Tran supports universal background checks and a ban on assault weapons. He also says he supports putting more police on the streets and investing in violence prevention and rehabilitation programs.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress

Foreign Policy.stance: Tran says he will stand up to the Chinese Communist Party, support democracy and human rights, strengthen alliances, and reduce reliance on Chinese supply chains. His campaign also says he supports strategic diplomacy and measures to counter Chinese aggression.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress
H.R. 7969 Combating Chinese Communist Party Influence Act

Social Justice.stance: Tran says he will defend rights and equal protections, oppose discrimination against LGBTQ+, immigrant, and minority communities, and strengthen voting rights and free and fair elections.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress

Education.stance: Tran says he supports raising teacher wages, investing in public schools, expanding early childhood education, and increasing vocational education and job training.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress
Representative Tran Defends Public Education, Demands Release of Critical Funding

Tech & AI.stance: Tran says the United States should lead in developing new technologies and that blockchain and crypto should have clear regulations that foster growth while protecting consumers and national security. No separate AI-specific position was found on his campaign site.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress

Local Issues.stance: Tran says he supports local infrastructure and public safety investments in California’s 45th District, along with small-business support and affordable housing. His House office also highlights community project funding requests and district services.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress
About | Representative Derek Tran - House.gov

Immigration.stance: Tran has said he supports immigrant communities and has highlighted his family’s refugee background. His campaign and House materials emphasize protecting immigrants’ rights and providing resources such as Know Your Rights guidance.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress
Community Resources | Representative Derek Tran

Election Reform.stance: Tran says he supports strengthening voting rights, defending free and fair elections, and campaign finance reforms that limit the influence of lobbyists and special interests.

sources.title: Issues — Derek Tran For Congress

candidates.name: Michelle Steel
candidates.summary: Michelle Steel is a Republican former U.S. representative from Orange County, California, who served in the U.S. House from 2021 to 2025 after earlier service on the Orange County Board of Supervisors and the California State Board of Equalization. In August 2025, the Orange County Register reported that she would not run for Congress in 2026, so her 2026 candidacy status should be treated as not active unless later filings or announcements show otherwise.

summary_sources.title: Former Rep. Michelle Steel won't run for Congress in 2026

career_history.title: Orange County Supervisor
career_history.description: Served on the county board before election to Congress.

career_history.title: California State Board of Equalization member
career_history.description: Served in statewide tax administration office.

candidates.donor_summary: Raised approximately $10.45M in the 2023–2024 cycle, with the largest industry support coming from retired donors ($1,598,881), followed by Republican/conservative interests ($725,209), leadership PACs ($536,409), securities & investment ($515,392), and real estate ($440,451). The top disclosed contributor was the American Israel Public Affairs Committee at $143,187, and large individual contributions accounted for 27.75% of funds. Source data is available on OpenSecrets and the FEC candidate page.
candidates.voting_summary: Steel is a former U.S. Representative who served from Jan. 3, 2023 to Jan. 3, 2025. GovTrack’s 2024 report card says she introduced 47 bills and resolutions, got bipartisan cosponsors on 28 of 47 bills, had 8 bills reach the floor, and missed 1.3% of votes (16 of 1,241) in the 118th Congress; notable bills included the Telehealth Expansion Act of 2023, Solidify Iran Sanctions Act, and DETERRENT Act.

links.title: Rep. Michelle Steel's 2024 Report Card - GovTrack.us
Rep. Michelle Steel - Campaign Finance Summary - OpenSecrets

Healthcare.stance: Supports market-based health care approaches and has opposed single-payer proposals. Her public campaign materials and issue coverage emphasize lowering costs and expanding access without expanding a government-run system.

sources.title: 2024 California CA-45 US House of Representatives Voters Guide
Michelle Steel, Jay Chen on abortion, immigration, inflation

Economy.stance: Advocates lower taxes, restrained federal spending, and pro-growth policies aimed at reducing inflation and supporting domestic manufacturing. Her record and public statements also show opposition to some major federal spending and industrial-policy measures.

sources.title: Michelle Steel: A look back at what we accomplished in Congress in 2023
CHIPS and Science Act (H.R. 4346) — Congress.gov
Reminder: Steel Voted Against Lowering Costs, Creating Jobs and Spurring Innovation

Reproductive Rights.stance: Has taken an anti-abortion position and previously co-sponsored the Life at Conception Act, though she later rescinded that cosponsorship while saying she did not support federal restrictions on IVF. Her public record still places her on the anti-abortion side of the issue.

sources.title: GOP Rep. Michelle Steel rescinds her cosponsorship of Life at Conception Act
Michelle Steel's Congressional Scorecard by Reproductive Freedom for All

Immigration.stance: Supports stricter border security and enforcement-focused immigration policy. She has opposed granting citizenship to people who entered the country illegally and has framed immigration as a border-security issue.

sources.title: Rep. Michelle Steel, Jay Chen on abortion, immigration, inflation
Michelle Steel border crisis uniquely impacts Southern California

Guns & Safety.stance: No separate public campaign position was found in the sources reviewed. Her congressional record includes votes on major House legislation, but no clearly documented campaign statement on gun policy was identified here.

sources.title: Rep. Michelle Steel's 2024 Report Card - GovTrack.us
Representative Michelle Steel (S001135) - Congress.gov

Foreign Policy.stance: Supports a national-security-focused foreign policy that emphasizes countering China, backing U.S. alliances, and prioritizing border security as part of broader security policy. Her campaign materials and public comments have highlighted Taiwan and opposition to the Chinese Communist Party.

sources.title: What I Stand For | Michelle Steel for U.S. Congress
Michelle Steel: A look back at what we accomplished in Congress in 2023

Social Justice.stance: No separate public campaign position was found in the sources reviewed. Her congressional record includes votes on major House legislation, but this profile does not identify a specific public statement on civil rights, LGBTQ+ policy, or related social-justice issues.

sources.title: Rep. Michelle Steel's 2024 Report Card - GovTrack.us
Representative Michelle Steel (S001135) - Congress.gov

Education.stance: Supports higher-education transparency and efforts to limit foreign influence in colleges and universities. She backed measures such as the DETERRENT Act, which focused on reporting foreign gifts and curbing foreign propaganda in higher education.

sources.title: Michelle Steel: A look back at what we accomplished in Congress in 2023

Election Reform.stance: Supports proof-of-citizenship requirements for federal voter registration and has aligned with measures that would tighten election administration rules. Her public record also includes support for voting-related restrictions in House votes.

sources.title: Michelle Steel - League of Conservation Voters
Rep. Michelle Steel - Scorecard 118: 60% | Heritage Action

Climate/Energy.stance: Her voting record and outside scorecards indicate a generally anti-regulatory, pro-fossil-fuel posture on climate and energy issues, including opposition to many clean-energy and emissions-related measures. She has also been criticized by environmental groups for opposing climate and clean-energy priorities.

sources.title: Michelle Steel - League of Conservation Voters
Michelle Steel | LCV Victory Fund

flags.concern: The summary states Steel 'would not run for Congress in 2026' based on an August 2025 Orange County Register report, which correctly represents the source. However, the summary also includes the parenthetical caveat that 'her 2026 candidacy status should be treated as not active unless later filings or announcements show otherwise.' This is prudent editorial language, but the summary itself correctly reflects the cited source and is internally consistent.

Steel's Healthcare stance is sourced to a 2024 voters' guide and a 2022 LA Times article. The confidence is marked 'medium,' which is appropriate given the temporal distance and reliance on secondary coverage rather than a primary campaign statement. The sourcing is adequate but not as comprehensive as Tran's direct campaign-site sourcing.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding Steel's primary campaign materials (michellesteelca.com/what-i-stand-for/) if healthcare detail appears there, to strengthen primary-source grounding. Current sourcing is acceptable.

flags.concern: Marked as 'no public position found' with low confidence (low). While the profile acknowledges the gap transparently and cites GovTrack and Congress.gov, this reflects a genuine research limitation rather than a profile error. The candidate is not obscure, but gun policy may not have been a central campaign or floor focus.
flags.suggestion: This gap is acceptable and properly documented. No revision needed.

flags.concern: Marked as low confidence with no public position found. Like the Guns & Safety gap, this is transparently documented. For a Republican incumbent from Orange County (2021–2025), social-justice positions may not have been prominently featured in primary sources.
flags.suggestion: Gap is properly labeled and documented. No revision needed.

flags.concern: Marked 'No public position found' with low confidence. This is consistent with the profile's transparent gap-documentation approach and does not represent an error.

Marked 'No public position found' with low confidence. Given that Steel served the district from 2021–2025, this gap is somewhat notable, though it may reflect a research scope that focused on national/statewide issues rather than district-specific statements.
flags.suggestion: Consider supplementing with any archived district office materials or past constituent services statements if available; however, the gap is appropriately documented.

flags.concern: Derek Tran's career history lists 'Attorney' with organization 'Private practice,' start_year null, and end_year 2025. This is internally consistent with his summary describing him as an 'attorney' but reflects a lack of specific years. Given his official House biography is cited as a source, this minor data sparsity is acceptable.

Only one poll is included: USC/Cal State Long Beach/Cal Poly Pomona from Sept. 24, 2024, showing Tran 51.0% vs. Steel 49.5%. The profile also states in polling_note that 'No public polling found for this race in the last 90 days as of 2026-04-07.' This is internally consistent and appropriately disclaims the recency of the single poll. The 2024 poll predates Steel's August 2025 withdrawal, making it of historical rather than predictive value.

reviews.summary: This profile is well-sourced and factually accurate across both candidates. Derek Tran's summary correctly reflects his House tenure (began 2025) and career background, with strong official sources from House.gov, Congress.gov, and his campaign site. Michelle Steel's summary appropriately notes her August 2025 decision not to run in 2026, citing the Orange County Register report. Issue positions are consistently backed by official campaign pages, legislative records, and credible news outlets. The profile demonstrates good-faith research effort for both candidates, including appropriately low-confidence ratings where public positions are unavailable (e.g., Steel on guns, social justice, local issues). Voting records are sourced to GovTrack and tied to actual legislative records. Language throughout is neutral and nonpartisan. Minor weaknesses: some issue sections for Steel show limited sourcing depth, and a few scorecard sources (e.g., Heritage Action) could be supplemented with additional independent verification. Overall, this is a solid, usable profile for a 2026 race with clear incumbent-vs.-non-incumbent distinction.

flags.concern: The percentages (51.0 and 49.5) sum to 100.5%. This may be due to rounding in the original source or the inclusion of a margin of error overlap.
flags.suggestion: Verify the specific poll figures against the USC/CSULB source URL to ensure no data entry error occurred.

flags.concern: The profile lists Derek Tran as an incumbent with a voting record starting in January 2025. This assumes a 2024 election victory. While consistent with the cited House.gov and GovTrack links, this reflects a timeline subsequent to my training data's cutoff.
flags.suggestion: Human reviewers should confirm that the 'tran.house.gov' and GovTrack links are active and reflect the current holder of the CA-45 seat as of the 2026 cycle.

reviews.summary: The profile is exceptionally well-sourced and internally consistent, demonstrating a high level of research depth. It effectively utilizes primary sources—including House.gov biographies, GovTrack voting records, and FEC/OpenSecrets financial data—to build a credible snapshot of the 2026 landscape. The report handles the shift in incumbency with precision and provides a nuanced account of Michelle Steel's status by citing specific local news reporting from the Orange County Register. Issue stances are framed neutrally, using the candidates' own language and official policy pages.

This profile is a well-constructed and reliable resource, featuring strong sourcing from authoritative sites like Congress.gov, OpenSecrets, and reputable news outlets such as the Orange County Register and LA Times, which directly support claims across all issues and candidate backgrounds. It maintains a neutral tone throughout, accurately documents gaps in public positions (e.g., for Michelle Steel on Tech & AI), and provides detailed career histories with consistent internal references, making it an excellent example of thorough, unbiased research.

validation_grade.summary: Validated by 3/3 reviewers with an average score of 93/100.
